rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference visible ports, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use valid logical operators (and, or, not, =>) and follow AADL scope rules for component features and data types.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Assumptions and guarantees should relate visible inputs to outputs, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in type-level contracts.
Ensure scalar type declarations in AGREE use int, real, or bool; avoid invalid or mismatched types that cause resolution errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should use valid AADL feature names and data types.
Use `const` for named constants and `eq` for helper expressions; ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-level paths in component type annexes; move such bindings to implementation scopes.
Avoid generating clauses that compare event ports to numeric or Boolean values unless explicit data representations exist in the model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and configured dependencies beyond the current model scope
Model validator diagnostics or repair actions
Specific requirement item IDs or textual descriptions
